CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
JOHN DAVID ELLIS self-represented plaintiff
Amanda Villalobos, Esq., of TUCKER ELLIS LLP. Tel:(213)430.3400 for defendant Progressive
Advantage.

Hearing held.A timely Notice of Contest was received from defendant's counsel regarding Plaintiff has alleged he entered an insurance contract with Demurring Defendant. The matter argued. The Court, having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Progressive Advantage Agency, Inc.'s Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED.

(see word minutes for complete detail)

Order sign.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
John Ellis 415-806-7864 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Brian Davis, Esq. 415-421-1100 DKM Law Group for Defendant Homesite.
Amanda Villalobos, Esq. 213-430-3395 Tucker Ellis LLP for Defendant Progressive

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Ms. Villalobos agrees with TR.
Mr. Ellis contests and presents arguments.
The Court comments and poses questions.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Progressive Corporation's 's motion to quash.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE ADVANTAGE AGENCY, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Progressive Advantage Agency, Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff John David Ellis's complaint is OFF CALENDAR.

Defendant has not demonstrated its moving papers were timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Defendant served its moving papers on Defendant by mail on 3/17/2026, which is not 16 court days plus five days before 4/13/2026, the hearing date noticed. Service by email was improper because Plaintiff is unrepresented and did not consent to electronic service. (See Code Civ. Proc. 1010.6, subd. (c).)

Further, the parties did not timely meet and confer in satisfaction of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41, subdivision (a), which requires that the parties meet and confer "at least 5 days before the date the responsive pleading is due." (Code Civ. Proc. 1010.6, subd. (a).) The clear legislative intent is that the conference involve personal interaction and be conducted at a time that allows meaningful consideration of the other side's issues. Defense counsel's declaration states that the parties did not meet until March 17, 2026 - the same day Defendant's responsive pleading was due and the same day it filed its demurrer. (See Villalobos Decl. 2.)

Defendant has 15 days leave to respond to the complaint; time running from notice of this order.

Prior to the hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
